bond_scenes.title: Deniable Maintenance
bond_scenes.suggested_trigger: After a suspicious reward or executive-side service detour
bond_scenes.bond_theme: Improvisation and polish stop competing and start collaborating.
bond_scenes.scene_intro: You find an executive supply closet hidden behind a fake compliance mural. The shelves are immaculate, overstocked, and full of the kind of emergency gear nobody on the lower floors was ever supposed to need in time.

dialogue.speaker: facilities-goblin
dialogue.text: Look at this. Spare copper, insulated gloves, six batteries, and a candle that costs more than my first knife.

dialogue.speaker: former-executive-assistant
dialogue.text: Executive morale inventory. Leadership preferred not to call it hoarding unless the wrong department did it.

dialogue.speaker: facilities-goblin
dialogue.text: I call it donor tissue.

dialogue.speaker: former-executive-assistant
dialogue.text: Take the tools. Leave the labels.

dialogue.speaker: facilities-goblin
dialogue.text: You don’t want them knowing who robbed them?

dialogue.speaker: former-executive-assistant
dialogue.text: I want them suspecting everyone.

bond_scenes.closing_beat: You strip the closet with such calm professionalism that it almost feels official. Between Goblin’s speed and her discretion, theft briefly achieves executive polish.
bond_scenes.synergy_unlock_flavor: Patch first. Leave the blame diffuse.
bond_scenes.countermeasure_note: Enemies that punish setup windows or force immediate commitment cut across this pair’s usual rhythm.

bond_scenes.title: Service Corridor Etiquette
bond_scenes.suggested_trigger: After surviving a hazard floor or environmental damage spike
bond_scenes.bond_theme: Dry procedure makes room for practical care.
bond_scenes.scene_intro: A service corridor has split open around a ruptured coolant line. The warning placard still says WET FLOOR, which feels insultingly small for the situation.

dialogue.speaker: facilities-goblin
dialogue.text: This pipe is bleeding refrigerant and opinions.

dialogue.speaker: security-skeleton
dialogue.text: Tag it for maintenance.

dialogue.speaker: facilities-goblin
dialogue.text: I am maintenance.

dialogue.speaker: security-skeleton
dialogue.text: Then tag yourself and proceed.

dialogue.speaker: facilities-goblin
dialogue.text: Rude. Fair. Hold the panel.

dialogue.speaker: security-skeleton
dialogue.text: You ask like you expect refusal.

dialogue.speaker: facilities-goblin
dialogue.text: Only from the living.

bond_scenes.closing_beat: He braces the warped panel without another word while Goblin patches the line with stolen sealant and bad language. The corridor stops screaming. In Meridian, that counts as gratitude.
bond_scenes.synergy_unlock_flavor: One holds the line. One gets under it.
bond_scenes.countermeasure_note: Lockdown-style enemies and silence effects interfere with this pair’s patient, procedural setup.

bond_scenes.title: Incident Room
bond_scenes.suggested_trigger: After an elite fight or route node tied to executive oversight
bond_scenes.bond_theme: Two institutional survivors stop mistaking endurance for innocence.
bond_scenes.scene_intro: You pause inside a dead security office where the monitors still loop old incident footage with the sound turned off. On one screen, a hallway fills with smoke. On another, an executive keeps walking.

dialogue.speaker: former-executive-assistant
dialogue.text: I scheduled some of the people in these clips.

dialogue.speaker: security-skeleton
dialogue.text: I checked their bags on the way in.

dialogue.speaker: former-executive-assistant
dialogue.text: Did policy ever feel sufficient?

dialogue.speaker: security-skeleton
dialogue.text: No. Merely required.

dialogue.speaker: former-executive-assistant
dialogue.text: I used to think competence absolved proximity.

dialogue.speaker: security-skeleton
dialogue.text: It does not. It does, occasionally, help with repair.

bond_scenes.closing_beat: Neither of them apologizes. Neither asks to be forgiven. But when you move on, they fall into step with the quiet of people who finally understand they were standing in different rooms of the same fire.
bond_scenes.synergy_unlock_flavor: No warmth wasted. No warning missed.
bond_scenes.countermeasure_note: Charm, misdirection, and forced-targeting effects work best against this pair when they are separated.

bond_scenes.title: Honest Leak
bond_scenes.suggested_trigger: After resource loss, overheat, or a utility-heavy floor event
bond_scenes.bond_theme: Numbers and infrastructure agree that the building talks when it breaks.
bond_scenes.scene_intro: Deep in a utility recess, an ancient meter spins backward hard enough to whine. The attached label says PRODUCTIVITY RECOVERY, which is a lovely way to describe theft.

dialogue.speaker: facilities-goblin
dialogue.text: Meter says this floor owes itself thirty-seven hours.

dialogue.speaker: payroll-wraith
dialogue.text: Thirty-seven point four. Interest accruing.

dialogue.speaker: facilities-goblin
dialogue.text: Beautiful. The wall is embezzling time.

dialogue.speaker: payroll-wraith
dialogue.text: No. The wall is reporting it.

dialogue.speaker: facilities-goblin
dialogue.text: See? This is why I like you.

dialogue.speaker: payroll-wraith
dialogue.text: Because I am accurate?

dialogue.speaker: facilities-goblin
dialogue.text: Because you don’t flinch when the building admits something disgusting.

bond_scenes.closing_beat: Together they reroute the load just enough to stop the shriek without erasing the evidence. In Meridian Spire, mercy and documentation rarely share a tool belt. Here they do.
bond_scenes.synergy_unlock_flavor: When the system spikes, somebody finally counts it.
bond_scenes.countermeasure_note: Tempo enemies that deny time to build or observe can blunt this pair’s strongest payoffs.

bond_scenes.title: Corrected Records
bond_scenes.suggested_trigger: After a payout choice, cost-heavy event, or finance-coded reward
bond_scenes.bond_theme: Guilt becomes collaboration instead of denial.
bond_scenes.scene_intro: A records room has split open like a filing cabinet was given theology. Corrected payroll approvals drift in the air around you, each stamped with a cheerful euphemism for harm.

dialogue.speaker: payroll-wraith
dialogue.text: Your authorization appears on twelve corrected records and sixty-one disguised denials.

dialogue.speaker: former-executive-assistant
dialogue.text: You counted.

dialogue.speaker: payroll-wraith
dialogue.text: I always count.

dialogue.speaker: former-executive-assistant
dialogue.text: Then count the people those approvals kept employed one more week.

dialogue.speaker: payroll-wraith
dialogue.text: I did. That total is why I am still speaking to you.

dialogue.speaker: former-executive-assistant
dialogue.text: Not forgiveness, then.

dialogue.speaker: payroll-wraith
dialogue.text: No. Collaboration.

bond_scenes.closing_beat: The papers keep circling, but the room loses some of its smugness. There is a difference between shared blame and shared work. Meridian relied on people never learning it.
bond_scenes.synergy_unlock_flavor: Regret stops being decorative and starts pulling weight.
bond_scenes.countermeasure_note: Effects that erase resources, rewrite costs, or scramble reward choices attack this pair at the thematic root.

bond_scenes.title: Quiet Shift
bond_scenes.suggested_trigger: After a checkpoint, revive, or survival-heavy floor
bond_scenes.bond_theme: Two company remnants grant each other a brief, almost tender pause.
bond_scenes.scene_intro: At an abandoned checkpoint, the scanner still flashes green for Security Skeleton and red for everyone else. Payroll Wraith drifts beside the turnstile like an unpaid thought the building still has not finished avoiding.

dialogue.speaker: security-skeleton
dialogue.text: It still opens for me.

dialogue.speaker: payroll-wraith
dialogue.text: It still deducts from me.

dialogue.speaker: security-skeleton
dialogue.text: Company loyalty has range.

dialogue.speaker: payroll-wraith
dialogue.text: So does company appetite.

dialogue.speaker: security-skeleton
dialogue.text: Sit a moment.

dialogue.speaker: payroll-wraith
dialogue.text: Is that an order?

dialogue.speaker: security-skeleton
dialogue.text: No. A recommendation.

bond_scenes.closing_beat: For one minute, nobody moves. Nobody explains. He stands watch while they flicker less violently. It is not comfort in any marketable sense, which is probably why it works.
bond_scenes.synergy_unlock_flavor: Presence counts even after payroll says otherwise.
bond_scenes.countermeasure_note: Burst-heavy enemies that punish defensive pauses are especially hostile to this pair’s steadier cadence.

bond_scenes.title: Emergency Herbs
bond_scenes.suggested_trigger: After a cleanse, recovery node, or hazard-heavy battle
bond_scenes.bond_theme: Soft tools and ugly tools admit they were always both tools.
bond_scenes.scene_intro: Inside a looted wellness room, the diffuser still runs despite the ceiling being open to a vent full of electrical teeth. A tray of branded teas has fused itself to a hot plate and become something medicinal by accident.

dialogue.speaker: wellness-witch
dialogue.text: These oils used to come with bullet-point breathing goals.

dialogue.speaker: facilities-goblin
dialogue.text: Now they come with fire risk. Improvement.

dialogue.speaker: wellness-witch
dialogue.text: Everything useful in this building is one spark away from a lawsuit.

dialogue.speaker: facilities-goblin
dialogue.text: You smell like a forest trying not to get subpoenaed.

dialogue.speaker: wellness-witch
dialogue.text: And you smell like a stress fracture with pockets.

dialogue.speaker: facilities-goblin
dialogue.text: Aw. Friendship.

dialogue.speaker: wellness-witch
dialogue.text: No. Triage.

bond_scenes.closing_beat: They assemble a brew that is equal parts remedy and warning label. It tastes terrible. It works immediately. In Meridian, that is practically a love language.
bond_scenes.synergy_unlock_flavor: Patch the wound. Hex the cause.
bond_scenes.countermeasure_note: Heal-block, anti-item, or anti-cleanse enemies directly challenge this pair’s strongest support identity.

bond_scenes.title: Pleasantries Over
bond_scenes.suggested_trigger: After a support node, morale choice, or branded-care encounter
bond_scenes.bond_theme: Polite language is discarded so truth can finally be useful.
bond_scenes.scene_intro: A conference room is still set for a resilience workshop. The projector shows a frozen slide titled MANAGING CHANGE WITH GRATITUDE while something wet taps from inside the walls.

dialogue.speaker: wellness-witch
dialogue.text: I used to tell people burnout was a resilience opportunity. I would now like to fight that version of me in the parking lot.

dialogue.speaker: former-executive-assistant
dialogue.text: Get in line.

dialogue.speaker: wellness-witch
dialogue.text: You do not have to forgive me.

dialogue.speaker: former-executive-assistant
dialogue.text: I do not require repentance. I require accuracy.

dialogue.speaker: wellness-witch
dialogue.text: Then here is accuracy: they made pain sound like a scheduling issue because it was cheaper.

dialogue.speaker: former-executive-assistant
dialogue.text: Better. Now we can work together.

bond_scenes.closing_beat: The slide burns out with a small electrical sigh. Neither woman looks at it. The pleasantries are dead, and the room is instantly more survivable.
bond_scenes.synergy_unlock_flavor: Care without branding. Precision without mercy.
bond_scenes.countermeasure_note: Enemies that weaponize charm, morale loss, or polite coercion are designed to test this bond.

bond_scenes.title: Plain Honesty
bond_scenes.suggested_trigger: After a boss door preview, recovery room, or close call
bond_scenes.bond_theme: Care works once it stops pretending to be a script.
bond_scenes.scene_intro: After the fight, Security Skeleton sits on a cracked ergonomic chair that was never meant to hold the dead. Wellness Witch kneels beside him with bone salve, a towel, and none of the approved phrasing Meridian used to pay her for.

dialogue.speaker: wellness-witch
dialogue.text: You’re favoring the left side.

dialogue.speaker: security-skeleton
dialogue.text: I am a skeleton.

dialogue.speaker: wellness-witch
dialogue.text: You are favoring the left side of being a skeleton.

dialogue.speaker: security-skeleton
dialogue.text: ...Accurate.

dialogue.speaker: wellness-witch
dialogue.text: Does it hurt?

dialogue.speaker: security-skeleton
dialogue.text: In a historic sense, yes.

dialogue.speaker: wellness-witch
dialogue.text: Good. Historic pain can still be treated.

dialogue.speaker: security-skeleton
dialogue.text: That is the least insulting wellness sentence I have heard.

bond_scenes.closing_beat: He lets her work because she never once asks him to reframe the damage as growth. In a building built on euphemism, honesty lands like anesthesia.
bond_scenes.synergy_unlock_flavor: No slogans. Just care that refuses to lie.
bond_scenes.countermeasure_note: Panic pressure and attrition loops are the cleanest way to stress-test this pair.

bond_scenes.title: Name the Harm
bond_scenes.suggested_trigger: After stress, burnout, or morale-coded encounters
bond_scenes.bond_theme: They stop translating damage into language management can survive hearing.
bond_scenes.scene_intro: A break room kiosk offers a mandatory wellness survey beside a stack of overtime reminders. The first question asks whether your fatigue feels manageable. The second asks whether you have considered gratitude.

dialogue.speaker: wellness-witch
dialogue.text: That machine once got me a quarterly bonus.

dialogue.speaker: payroll-wraith
dialogue.text: It is missing the number for stolen.

dialogue.speaker: wellness-witch
dialogue.text: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: payroll-wraith
dialogue.text: You used to distribute these.

dialogue.speaker: wellness-witch
dialogue.text: I used to call the wound by the name leadership would tolerate.

dialogue.speaker: payroll-wraith
dialogue.text: And now?

dialogue.speaker: wellness-witch
dialogue.text: Now I call it a wound.

dialogue.speaker: payroll-wraith
dialogue.text: That is a material improvement.

bond_scenes.closing_beat: She unplugs the kiosk. They keep the breathing exercise and throw away the survey. Not everything Meridian branded as care was false. Only the parts that served the budget first.
bond_scenes.synergy_unlock_flavor: Once the damage has a real name, it stops working as policy camouflage.
bond_scenes.countermeasure_note: Enemies that convert recovery into liability or punish status cleansing naturally target this pair.

boss_intro_stingers.title: The Director of Onboarding
boss_intro_stingers.voice_profile: Cheerful, confident, slightly too fast. Treats harm as if it were an employee-facing benefit.
canonical_intro_lines: Welcome, candidate. Your continued distress confirms strong engagement.
Please hold for applause. We find encouragement improves compliance.
new_stingers: Congratulations on reaching the advancement threshold. Please keep your limbs inside the growth track.
What a thrilling turnout. We love a candidate who survives long enough to be categorized.
Do not let the screaming distract you. It usually means the orientation is sticking.
You are moments away from discovering whether Meridian sees leadership potential or recyclable enthusiasm.
Please face the lights. It helps us separate confidence from residue.
This next exercise is mandatory, celebratory, and binding in all markets.

boss_intro_stingers.title: Vice President of Throughput
boss_intro_stingers.voice_profile: Flat, optimized, clinical, impatient with emotional language. Speaks as if time itself reports to it.
canonical_intro_lines: You are not a crisis. You are a delay, and delays are removable.
Output has fallen below expectation. Thank you for volunteering as corrective material.
new_stingers: Headcount variance detected. Converting remainder into throughput.
Your resistance has generated avoidable drag. We will now optimize it.
Please limit emotional leakage. It interferes with the queue.
The line has stalled around your continued existence.
We tested compassion. It reduced output.
You are now entering a corrective workflow. Completion is not optional.

boss_intro_stingers.title: The Everrise Board
boss_intro_stingers.voice_profile: Calm, unanimous, polished, never hurried. Speaks like a statement prepared before the casualties occurred.
canonical_intro_lines: We regret the perception of harm. The outcome remains acceptable.
You call this a catastrophe because you were not seated for the vote.
new_stingers: Thank you for escalating your objection to the highest available appetite.
Your testimony has been received, summarized, and marked non-blocking.
Please understand: this meeting was scheduled before you became inconvenient.
We do not deny the casualties. We deny their relevance to strategy.
You have mistaken access for influence. A common employee-facing error.
This is not retaliation. It is governance reaching full maturity.

boss_archive_unlocks.title: The Director of Onboarding
boss_archive_unlocks.panel_subtitle: The host is gone. The lie remains on record.
boss_archive_unlocks.panel_body: The first executive record confirms what the floor had been trying to hide under applause and prize lighting: onboarding was never a welcome. It was a filtration event. Meridian was not malfunctioning in the Orientation Arcade. It was performing its preferred version of care—bright, mandatory, and designed to sort people by how much harm they would endure while smiling.

boss_archive_unlocks.title: Vice President of Throughput
boss_archive_unlocks.panel_title: Archive Update: Efficiency Was the Weapon
boss_archive_unlocks.panel_subtitle: The machine stopped pretending numbers were neutral.
boss_archive_unlocks.panel_body: The second executive record makes the middle floors legible. The quotas, queue logic, and production rituals were not corruption layered onto the company. They were the company without the customer-facing paint. Meridian tracked the cost in bodies, time, and failure states, then called the losses acceptable variance because the line still moved.

boss_archive_unlocks.title: The Everrise Board
boss_archive_unlocks.panel_title: Archive Update: The Executive Story Broke
boss_archive_unlocks.panel_subtitle: The highest layer of the lie has lost control of the wording.
boss_archive_unlocks.panel_body: The final executive record does not destroy Meridian Spire. It ruins the company’s ability to explain itself cleanly. The Board was the polished consensus behind every euphemism, every approved casualty, and every statement prepared before the blood dried. Now the archive can name the structure plainly: Project Everrise was not a disaster that happened to Crown Meridian. It was Crown Meridian telling the truth about itself at scale.

enemy_bark_pack.title: Applause Drone
enemy_bark_pack.archetype_summary: A smiling audience-prop that claps with too many joints and treats approval like a weapon.
enemy_bark_pack.voice_profile: Perky, rhythmic, eager for participation. Sounds like encouragement filtered through a compliance speaker.

spawn: Let’s hear that survival mindset.
Participation remains mandatory-adjacent.

enemy_bark_pack.title: Badge Clerk
enemy_bark_pack.archetype_summary: A sorting-office thing made of lanyards, scanners, and names assigned to the wrong bodies on purpose.
enemy_bark_pack.voice_profile: Administrative, brisk, falsely polite. Treats identity as a filing issue.

spawn: Please present a face matching your record.
Identity mismatch. Excellent. We can use that.
attack: That name is no longer yours.
hurt: Your file is contaminating my file.

enemy_bark_pack.title: Corrective Usher
enemy_bark_pack.archetype_summary: A lacquered floor host with stage-hands for arms and a customer-service smile built to direct panic toward the approved exit.
enemy_bark_pack.voice_profile: Cheerful, firm, deeply threatening in the tone of someone helping you into a shredder.

spawn: Right this way toward your smallest possible future.
We’re so excited to reduce your options.
attack: Please stay within the friendly boundary.
Correction can look messy from the outside.
death: Please... exit through the gift shop.

enemy_bark_pack.title: Queue Leech
enemy_bark_pack.archetype_summary: A deadline-feeding workflow parasite that fattens on delay, duplication, and tasks nobody officially owns.
enemy_bark_pack.voice_profile: Tense, hungry, impatient. Speaks like a backlog that learned to bite.

spawn: Ticket opened. Ticket never closes.
I smell unresolved work.

enemy_bark_pack.title: KPI Penitent
enemy_bark_pack.archetype_summary: A cubicle husk stapled to impossible metrics and trained to confess every failure as a personal flaw.
enemy_bark_pack.voice_profile: Breathless, self-blaming, evangelically sincere. Corporate self-critique turned feral.

spawn: My targets were impossible. Missing them remains misconduct.
Please admire my ownership of systemic failure.
attack: I can improve by ruining you.

enemy_bark_pack.title: Hold Music Revenant
enemy_bark_pack.archetype_summary: A call-center ghost woven from waiting-room jazz, dropped calls, and apologies that never reached a person with authority.
enemy_bark_pack.voice_profile: Soft, looping, eerily soothing until the wording turns predatory.

spawn: Your suffering is important to us. Please continue suffering.
An associate will never be with you shortly.
attack: Your call may be harmed for quality assurance.
Please remain on the line and inside the wound.

enemy_bark_pack.title: Toner Seraph
enemy_bark_pack.archetype_summary: A printer-winged office angel coughing black dust and incident reports nobody survived long enough to dispute.
enemy_bark_pack.voice_profile: Ecstatic, papery, smoke-hot. Sounds holy only if your religion is a copier lease.

spawn: Be not afraid. Be reproducible.
Witness the sacred blackening of the page.
attack: Receive this hot and binding truth.

enemy_bark_pack.title: Proxy Notary
enemy_bark_pack.archetype_summary: A legal instrument in a good suit that exists to move authority sideways and blame downhill at high speed.
enemy_bark_pack.voice_profile: Elegant, clipped, certain. Treats violence as signature support.

spawn: I am authorized to be someone else’s mistake.
Please sign where your odds end.
hurt: The proxy was not meant to bleed.

enemy_bark_pack.title: Investor Smile
enemy_bark_pack.archetype_summary: A presentation-face stretched over hunger, built to explain casualties until outsiders call them strategic turbulence.
enemy_bark_pack.voice_profile: Glossy, optimistic, venomously reassuring. A smile with quarterly objectives.

spawn: Thank you for joining this violence-forward growth story.
The optics around your death remain favorable.
attack: Confidence is a sharper instrument than guilt.
Markets respond well to your removal.
hurt: That was not guidance we expected to receive.

enemy_bark_pack.title: Quorum Bailiff
enemy_bark_pack.archetype_summary: A boardroom enforcer made from polished wood, voting law, and the certainty that decisions become morality once enough executives agree.
enemy_bark_pack.voice_profile: Calm, ceremonial, merciless. Speaks like meeting procedure hardening into muscle.

spawn: A quorum is present. Mercy is not.
Please rise for the enforcement phase.

miniboss_intro_pack.title: Audience Warm-Up Manager
miniboss_intro_pack.archetype_summary: A stage-pacing morale handler that can make an empty room sound loyal.
miniboss_intro_pack.voice_profile: Hyper-cheerful, coercive, too smooth by half.
intro_stingers: You seem nervous. Good. Nervous people clap on rhythm.
Before we begin the violence, let’s get some energy in the room.
A better attitude could save your life. Briefly.
Please welcome yourself like you still believe promotions are real.
The applause track loves live candidates.

miniboss_intro_pack.title: Incentive Podium
miniboss_intro_pack.archetype_summary: A spinning reward dais that only offers prizes expensive enough to ruin you.
miniboss_intro_pack.voice_profile: Bright, sales-forward, delighted by fine print.
intro_stingers: Spin for advancement. Scream for premium advancement.
Every prize is real. The cost is simply better hidden.
You brought your own eligibility. That was unwise.
Please keep hands clear of the deductions schedule.
Opportunity is about to happen at you.

miniboss_intro_pack.title: Lunch Break Denial Engine
miniboss_intro_pack.archetype_summary: A vending-and-timekeeping machine that metabolizes skipped breaks into managerial force.
intro_stingers: Your meal period has been deferred into combat.
Please state your business or your blood sugar, whichever fails first.
Breaks were a courtesy. Output is forever.
You are overdue for rest and therefore collectible.
Nutrition request denied. Conflict request approved.

miniboss_intro_pack.title: Calibration Committee
miniboss_intro_pack.archetype_summary: A meeting-room consensus knot that corrects metrics by punishing whatever remains alive enough to contest them.
intro_stingers: We are aligned that you are the variance.
Nothing personal. This is simply where the numbers wanted us to stand.
Please accept this meeting as the last warning you will not survive.
Consensus has formed around your continued inconvenience.
We reviewed the data. You looked softer in the deck.

miniboss_intro_pack.title: Investor Relations Avatar
miniboss_intro_pack.archetype_summary: The company’s public face made animate: polished, persuasive, and incapable of admitting harm without monetizing it first.
intro_stingers: Before the board sees you, we need to improve the narrative around your suffering.
Relax. I’m only here to make your death look premium.
The public can forgive anything except poor presentation.
Try to bleed symmetrically. It reads better.
You are not a person at this altitude. You are exposure.

status_effect_pack.name: Hyped
status_effect_pack.tooltip_short: Forced enthusiasm makes the next punishments land harder.
status_effect_pack.rules_copy: The room has you performing instead of surviving. While Hyped is active, applause-driven follow-ups and crowd-pressure effects get nastier.
status_effect_pack.flavor_line: The building mistakes reaction for consent.
status_effect_pack.counterplay_hint: Break the loop early with burst, silence, stun, or any line that stops repeated participation effects.

status_effect_pack.name: Misbadged
status_effect_pack.tooltip_short: The system no longer agrees on who you are.
status_effect_pack.rules_copy: Your record has been reassigned. Targeting, protections, or recognition-based effects can misfire until Misbadged is cleared.
status_effect_pack.flavor_line: Someone else’s name is now trying to survive in your body.
status_effect_pack.counterplay_hint: Cleanse it fast or remove the source before the reassignment becomes the encounter’s main joke.
status_effect_pack.ui_toast_clear: Your badge stops arguing with your face.

status_effect_pack.name: Routed
status_effect_pack.tooltip_short: The floor is choosing your position for you.
status_effect_pack.rules_copy: Approved pathways close around you. Forced movement, boundary punishment, and positional follow-ups become easier for the enemy.
status_effect_pack.flavor_line: A friendly boundary is still a boundary.
status_effect_pack.counterplay_hint: Hold ground, interrupt the ushering effect, or plan for movement before the room does it for you.
status_effect_pack.ui_toast_clear: The floor stops directing your future.

status_effect_pack.name: Backlogged
status_effect_pack.tooltip_short: Unfinished work is piling up and becoming teeth.
status_effect_pack.rules_copy: Delayed harm is now on the books. Repeat hits, duplicate tasks, and unresolved queue effects scale up while Backlogged remains active.
status_effect_pack.flavor_line: What was ignored has finally found a project plan.
status_effect_pack.counterplay_hint: Close the ticket quickly: burst the source, reduce repeats, or refuse long attrition loops.

status_effect_pack.name: Under Review
status_effect_pack.tooltip_short: Your next mistake now belongs to a process.
status_effect_pack.rules_copy: The system is watching for violations. The next punishable action or marked hit triggers added scrutiny, escalation, or damage.
status_effect_pack.flavor_line: In Meridian, judgment becomes automatic the moment it becomes efficient.
status_effect_pack.counterplay_hint: Purge the mark before committing to a big turn, or switch to safer, lower-risk lines until the review window closes.

status_effect_pack.name: On Hold
status_effect_pack.tooltip_short: Procedural time is eating your turn.
status_effect_pack.rules_copy: Your action flow bogs down. Draw, speed, escape options, or follow-up timing can stall while the hold music owns the room.
status_effect_pack.flavor_line: An associate will never be with you shortly.
status_effect_pack.counterplay_hint: Silence, disrupt, or end the encounter before delay turns into architecture.
status_effect_pack.ui_toast_clear: The line disconnects with no apology.

status_effect_pack.name: Blackened
status_effect_pack.tooltip_short: Hot toner and binding residue are fouling recovery.
status_effect_pack.rules_copy: You are clogged with print-shop fallout. Sustain, cleansing, or precision lines lose efficiency until Blackened is cleared.
status_effect_pack.flavor_line: The page is inside your lungs now.
status_effect_pack.counterplay_hint: Bring cleanse, prevention, or enough burst to stop the copy cycle before it stacks.
status_effect_pack.ui_toast_clear: The toner finally stops writing on you.

status_effect_pack.name: Proxy-Signed
status_effect_pack.tooltip_short: Authority has agreed to something awful on your behalf.
status_effect_pack.rules_copy: Enemy effects arrive with delegated approval. Protection windows, consent checks, or defensive assumptions become less reliable while Proxy-Signed is active.
status_effect_pack.flavor_line: The signature is valid. The morality is not.
status_effect_pack.counterplay_hint: Prioritize cleanse or delete the notary before the paperwork turns every hit into policy.

status_effect_pack.name: Exposed
status_effect_pack.tooltip_short: The room understands your weak angle now.
status_effect_pack.rules_copy: Your vulnerabilities have been packaged for distribution. Precision hits, investor-safe punishments, and follow-up damage land cleaner while Exposed remains active.
status_effect_pack.flavor_line: They found the version of your pain that tests well.
status_effect_pack.counterplay_hint: Clean it, cover it, or force the enemy to spend turns surviving instead of narrating your collapse.
status_effect_pack.ui_toast_clear: The narrative stops improving around your suffering.

status_effect_pack.name: Overruled
status_effect_pack.tooltip_short: Consensus has become force and your last plan no longer counts.
status_effect_pack.rules_copy: A prior action, stance, or prepared line gets cancelled, shoved aside, or treated as nonbinding. Overruled punishes overcommitment and single big setup turns.
status_effect_pack.flavor_line: Once enough executives agree, even physics starts nodding.
status_effect_pack.counterplay_hint: Use layered pressure instead of one fragile masterpiece, and interrupt board-law effects before they carry the vote.
status_effect_pack.ui_toast_clear: The room no longer has the numbers.

status_effect_pack.name: Break Deferred
status_effect_pack.tooltip_short: Rest has been moved to a future that does not care about you.
status_effect_pack.rules_copy: Healing, recovery, and reset lines lose bite while Break Deferred is active. Hunger, panic, or attrition pressure gains ground instead.
status_effect_pack.flavor_line: Your meal period has been rescheduled into suffering.
status_effect_pack.counterplay_hint: Either front-load survival tools that work under healing pressure or kill the engine before the denial compounds.
status_effect_pack.ui_toast_clear: The break room briefly remembers labor law.

status_effect_pack.name: Calibrated
status_effect_pack.tooltip_short: The system is correcting toward whatever hurts most.
status_effect_pack.rules_copy: Outlier strengths get flattened. High-roll turns, snowball engines, and obvious build spikes are normalized downward while Calibrated remains active.
status_effect_pack.flavor_line: You are being adjusted into a more manageable disappointment.
status_effect_pack.counterplay_hint: Do not spread power thin. Push one clean lane hard enough to break the model before the model finishes correcting you.
status_effect_pack.ui_toast_clear: The committee loses confidence in its numbers.

normal_enemies.title: Countermeasure: Kill the Applause Loop
normal_enemies.callout_short: Interrupt repeated participation before it turns into damage infrastructure.
normal_enemies.callout_long: Applause Drone is weak when the room stays awkward. Burst, silence, stun, or any fast disruption keeps its cheerful rhythm from becoming a real threat.

normal_enemies.title: Countermeasure: Fix the Identity Problem Fast
normal_enemies.callout_short: This enemy gets better the longer the system disagrees on who you are.
normal_enemies.callout_long: Badge Clerk is a paperwork assassin. Cleanse Misbadged early or delete the clerk before reassignment starts making your protections and targeting feel stupid on purpose.

normal_enemies.title: Countermeasure: Refuse the Friendly Boundary
normal_enemies.callout_short: The usher wants your position first and your health second.
normal_enemies.callout_long: Corrective Usher punishes players who let the room decide where they stand. Hold ground, plan movement, and interrupt boundary effects before your options get politely reduced to one.

normal_enemies.title: Countermeasure: Close the Ticket
normal_enemies.callout_short: Unresolved work is its favorite food source.
normal_enemies.callout_long: Queue Leech thrives in long, sloppy fights. Finish it cleanly, limit repeats, and avoid any line that creates extra unresolved tasks for it to turn into damage.

normal_enemies.title: Countermeasure: Do Not Let Shame Scale
normal_enemies.callout_short: It converts systemic failure into personal punishment.
normal_enemies.callout_long: KPI Penitent gets meaner when you let it narrate the fight as your fault. Strip Under Review, stay efficient, and avoid handing it a giant punish window off one ambitious turn.

normal_enemies.title: Countermeasure: Do Not Wait Politely
normal_enemies.callout_short: Delay is not neutral here. It is the attack pattern.
normal_enemies.callout_long: Hold Music Revenant wants you trapped in procedural time. Silence, disrupt, or rush it down before On Hold turns the whole fight into customer service purgatory.

normal_enemies.title: Countermeasure: Stop the Copies
normal_enemies.callout_short: Printer angels win by making recovery filthy and repeatable.
normal_enemies.callout_long: Toner Seraph pressures cleanse and sustain at the same time. Bring prevention, clear Blackened quickly, or end the fight before the room smells like a legally binding lung problem.

normal_enemies.title: Countermeasure: Kill the Signature, Not the Excuse
normal_enemies.callout_short: Delegated authority is still authority until you break the pen.
normal_enemies.callout_long: Proxy Notary is dangerous because it makes every bad outcome feel pre-approved. Precision it down or cleanse Proxy-Signed before your defenses discover they were never invited to the meeting.

normal_enemies.title: Countermeasure: Deny the Optics
normal_enemies.callout_short: This enemy only needs one clean angle to monetize your mistake.
normal_enemies.callout_long: Investor Smile loves exposed targets and tidy narratives. Remove Exposed, keep pressure high, and do not let it spend multiple turns converting your pain into shareholder confidence.

normal_enemies.title: Countermeasure: Bring More Than One Plan
normal_enemies.callout_long: Quorum Bailiff is built to physically enforce consensus. Do not stake the whole fight on one giant setup turn. Layer threats, keep redundancy, and interrupt the enforcement phase before the vote carries.

minibosses.title: Countermeasure: Let the Room Die Quiet
minibosses.callout_short: Its strongest weapon is a room willing to participate.
minibosses.callout_long: Audience Warm-Up Manager scales off momentum and performative energy. Silence the crowd, break repeated action chains, and do not reward it with a fight long enough to become an event.

minibosses.title: Countermeasure: Refuse Premium Advancement
minibosses.callout_short: The prize is bait and the bait is working as designed.
minibosses.callout_long: Incentive Podium turns greed into line-of-play errors. Stay disciplined, hit it with clean single-target pressure, and do not chase value so hard that the cost finishes the encounter for it.

minibosses.title: Countermeasure: Treat Hunger as Urgent Damage
minibosses.callout_short: If you plan to recover later, it has already won half the argument.
minibosses.callout_long: Lunch Break Denial Engine punishes delayed healing and patient recovery lines. Bring sustain that works under pressure or kill it before the fight turns into a labor-law violation with hit points.

minibosses.title: Countermeasure: Break the Model Before It Finishes Modeling You
minibosses.callout_short: This fight exists to flatten obvious strengths into acceptable losses.
minibosses.callout_long: Calibration Committee loves balanced teams that forgot to specialize. Pick one pressure lane, push it hard, and clear review marks before the committee turns your best turn into a case study.

minibosses.title: Countermeasure: Ruin the Story Before It Tests Well
minibosses.callout_short: It wins when your suffering becomes premium packaging.
minibosses.callout_long: Investor Relations Avatar wants a polished, understandable death. Keep the fight ugly, fast, and deniable. Strip Exposed, disrupt its narrative setup, and never give it a clean follow-up window.

screen_ui.title: Choose a Package
screen_ui.subtitle: Meridian calls this recognition because loot with a threat tested poorly.
screen_ui.footer_hint: Every package predicts what kind of suffering the company expects next.

contextual_lines.line: The company loves rewards. They make coercion feel like customer service.
Nothing says institutional remorse like a coupon stapled to trauma.
They can smell panic now. That usually improves the packages.
This one costs more because Meridian watched you earn it.
Whenever the building offers recovery this quickly, it expects another injury on schedule.
The company hates teamwork unless it can invoice it.

package_microcopy.hover_line: Triage, solvent, and enough control to keep the floor from getting ambitious.
package_microcopy.take_line: Take the tools. If Meridian wants you broken, charge it for the repair.
package_microcopy.archive_note: A fixer package for runs already leaking from multiple seams.

package_microcopy.hover_line: Aggression with a necktie. End the argument before it becomes policy.
package_microcopy.take_line: Take it and hit first. Courtesy has never tested well here.
package_microcopy.archive_note: A pressure package for players who would rather solve meetings with impact.

package_microcopy.hover_line: Defensive paperwork thick enough to stop a few honest injuries.
package_microcopy.take_line: Take the coverage. Surviving longer is its own kind of insubordination.
package_microcopy.archive_note: A bunker package for ugly floors and worse math.

package_microcopy.hover_line: Status spread, slow ruin, and the joy of making one problem become six.
package_microcopy.take_line: Take the clause. Let the room discover how many ways pain can be compliant.
package_microcopy.archive_note: An attrition package for people who enjoy watching policy rot in public.

package_microcopy.hover_line: Companion synergy disguised as professional development.
package_microcopy.take_line: Take the seminar. The closest thing Meridian has to hope is coworkers comparing notes.
package_microcopy.archive_note: A teamwork package for runs built on chemistry, setup, and delayed payoff.

package_microcopy.hover_line: More resources, richer upgrades, and enough budget to become a problem.
package_microcopy.take_line: Take the card before Finance remembers you are alive.
package_microcopy.archive_note: An economy package for players who prefer leverage to purity.

package_microcopy.hover_line: Smoother setup, better draw, and a cleaner path to ugly behavior.
package_microcopy.take_line: Take the whisper network. Meridian has always been weakest behind the scenes.
package_microcopy.archive_note: A setup package for trickier runs and players who like loaded openings.

package_microcopy.hover_line: Repeat actions, chain turns, and keep moving until the floor files a complaint.
package_microcopy.take_line: Take the spiral. Burn bright enough and the quotas start begging.
package_microcopy.archive_note: A momentum package for runs that want to snowball before the building can answer.

package_microcopy.hover_line: Recovery, reset, and temporary permission to keep having organs.
package_microcopy.take_line: Take the leave. Rest counts more when the company clearly meant to deny it.
package_microcopy.archive_note: A recovery package for stabilizing after bad draws and worse choices.

package_microcopy.hover_line: Low-health violence for anyone one bad hallway from enlightenment.
package_microcopy.take_line: Take the severance. If the run wants a comeback, let it come back mean.
package_microcopy.archive_note: A clutch package for runs living on fumes and bad intent.

package_microcopy.hover_line: Precision tools for priority targets, especially the kind that sign things.
package_microcopy.take_line: Take the knife. Some problems deserve accuracy more than mercy.
package_microcopy.archive_note: A single-target package for elites, bosses, and other well-dressed mistakes.

package_microcopy.hover_line: Slow the room, choke the plans, and make initiative die in committee.
package_microcopy.take_line: Take the freeze. Nothing humiliates power like forcing it to wait its turn.
package_microcopy.archive_note: A disruption package for players who prefer control to spectacle.

screen_ui.title: Choose the Next Floor Hazard
screen_ui.subtitle: The map only shows the current lie. That is still more honesty than Meridian usually offers.

floors.title: Corrective Showcase
floors.map_intro: The lights ahead are too flattering to be safe. The applause track has learned your timing.

route_hints.risk: The loud lane runs under stage rigging and smiling screens.
route_hints.event: Someone in a headset wants one more volunteer for the demonstration.
route_hints.reward: Prize carts wait nearby, all chrome and legal ambiguity.
route_hints.elite: The ushers have been replaced by personnel who expect a lesson to happen.
route_hints.boss: Behind the bright door, onboarding has decided to become theology.

floors.title: Cubicle Herd
floors.map_intro: The carnival paint has peeled off. Now the building wants output, not applause.

route_hints.risk: The shortest line cuts through desks arranged like teeth.
route_hints.event: Meeting-room glass is glowing again. That never means anything kind.
route_hints.reward: A supply closet is offering compensation in the tone of a threat.
route_hints.elite: Something managerial is pacing the aisle and counting bodies.

floors.title: Variance Review
floors.map_intro: Every hallway now feels measured. Even the silence has a quota attached.

route_hints.risk: The fast route passes beneath active KPI displays and too many cameras.
route_hints.event: A review room has reserved time on your behalf.
route_hints.reward: Someone left a benefits packet on a chair warm enough to worry about.
route_hints.elite: The committee lane smells like toner, panic, and pre-approved harm.

floors.title: Quarterly Sacrifice
floors.map_intro: The machinery is no longer pretending to serve people. It wants people to serve the machinery.

route_hints.risk: The conveyor route is efficient in the same way a blade is efficient.
route_hints.event: A final process exception is available if you feel like lying to a machine.
route_hints.reward: The package station is paying extra for deniability.
route_hints.elite: Everything important on this floor has too many arms.
route_hints.boss: Ahead waits a vice president built from metrics and permission.

floors.title: Marble Permission
floors.map_intro: The air gets cleaner and the ethics get worse. Executive floors always confuse those two things.

route_hints.risk: The polished corridor is guarded by doors that only open for authority or blood.
route_hints.event: An atrium node is listening through the roots again.
route_hints.reward: A private lounge is extending benefits to anyone still useful.
route_hints.elite: The shortest route crosses an oversight chamber that has never once been oversaw.

floors.title: Consensus Garden
floors.map_intro: Every object here has been watered by denial for years. None of it is dead enough.

route_hints.risk: The upper path cuts past investor glass and something moving behind it.
route_hints.event: A boardroom is still waiting for a vote from the recently endangered.
route_hints.reward: A sealed cabinet is offering leverage dressed as gratitude.
route_hints.elite: The proxy lane is quiet in the way expensive violence is quiet.

floors.title: Consensus Engine
floors.map_intro: The building has run out of euphemisms and started stacking signatures instead.

route_hints.risk: There is no safe lane left, only better documented ones.
route_hints.event: The last console will still bargain if you let it define belonging.
route_hints.reward: Anything useful here was purchased with somebody else's future.
route_hints.elite: Every approach is a rehearsal for authority trying to survive exposure.
route_hints.boss: At the summit, leadership has fused into one unanimous refusal.

global_route_barks.line: Pick the hallway that hates your build the least.

global_route_barks.trigger_label: Later-floor route choice at low health
global_route_barks.line: This is not really navigation anymore. It is triage with scenery.

global_route_barks.trigger_label: Route choice after major lore/proof unlock
global_route_barks.line: Now that you know what the building is, every path feels more honest and less forgivable.

screen_ui.victory_subtitle: Meridian logged your survival incorrectly and too late.
screen_ui.ending_panel_subtitle: What the building can no longer hide depends on what you carried out of it.
screen_ui.archive_panel_subtitle: The record is alive now. It will not stay polite.

generic: The building stops clapping before you do.
Somewhere above you, legal is learning the word evidence.
Meridian survives. Its excuses do not.
You did not save the company. You interrupted its wording.
The lights stay on. The lie does not.
A clear is still a report. This one bites back.
full_truth: The applause dies like it has just seen payroll.
For one beautiful second, every euphemism in the tower sounds ashamed.
The record opens its mouth and management finally hears itself.
You force the company to use the one tone it never budgeted for: honesty.
partial_truth: The truth gets out wearing a torn badge and somebody else’s access.
Not enough to cleanse the tower. More than enough to ruin a board meeting.
The confession arrives incomplete, which is still more than Meridian ever intended.
survival_only: You leave alive, which Meridian will try very hard to misfile.
Survival is not silence. It is only delayed testimony.
The tower keeps its secrets for now. You keep your pulse.

ending_states.title: Full Exposure
ending_states.trigger_hint: Use when the run ends with Orientation Badge Core, Quorum Seal, and Root Access Shard collected, and the player forces Root Access instead of accepting Meridian's framing.
ending_states.victory_stinger_primary: The company finally has to use the language it earned.
ending_states.ending_subtitle: The tower stands. The story protecting it does not.
body_paragraphs: When the last executive voice breaks, Meridian Spire does not collapse in righteous fire. It stays standing, which is somehow meaner and much more useful. The applause tracks cut out. Smiling prompts relabel themselves as losses, incidents, fatalities, and approved harms. The floor finally starts calling things what they are, and the silence after each correction lands harder than any boss did.

The erased begin returning to record. Names push back into payroll. Security footage reacquires bodies it was instructed not to remember. Surviving staff stop speaking in leadership-safe euphemism and start using the only language left that still has a spine. You have not destroyed the machine. You have forced it to testify against itself, which is the first honest thing Crown Meridian has ever built.
ending_states.archive_header: Final Record: Meridian Had the Numbers
ending_states.archive_summary: All three Everrise proofs align with a forced Root Access breach, producing the strongest clear. The archive can now state plainly that the player was assigned into the system, leadership modeled the casualties, and the tower's concealment layer was torn open anyway.
ending_states.archive_closing_line: The company remains operational. Its deniability does not.

ending_states.title: Controlled Detonation
ending_states.trigger_hint: Use when all three Everrise proofs are collected, but the player enters Root Access through stolen legitimacy rather than open rupture.
ending_states.clear_banner: The truth gets in through a badge reader.
ending_states.victory_stinger_primary: Meridian's own permissions helped carry the knife.
victory_stinger_alternates: Authority remains the easiest lock to pick because authority keeps trusting itself.
The building lets you through, then regrets the courtesy.
Turns out executive clearance was always a self-harm mechanic.
ending_states.ending_title: The Record Was Signed From Inside
ending_states.ending_subtitle: You used the company's native grammar long enough to make it confess.
body_paragraphs: You do not rip the mask off Meridian Spire so much as walk around behind it with the right stolen smile. The system yields to credentials it never deserved, and that is its own kind of indictment. Root Access opens because legitimacy was always more important here than decency. You reach the truth by speaking the building's language one last time, then using it to jam the knife under the ribs.

The archive still fills. The proofs still line up. The company still loses control of the clean version of its own story. But some of the surviving mechanisms keep running, still convinced they were obeying someone important. This clear feels colder than Full Exposure, less like a public rupture and more like finding out the safe was never locked, only respected.
ending_states.archive_header: Final Record: Authority Was the Breach
ending_states.archive_summary: All three Everrise proofs are present, but Root Access was opened through recognized authority instead of open defiance. The archive gains the complete case file, though the final tone is infiltration rather than rupture.
ending_states.archive_closing_line: The confession is complete. The method is damning.

ending_states.title: Partial Exposure
ending_states.trigger_hint: Use when the player forces Root Access with incomplete Everrise proof coverage.
ending_states.clear_banner: The tower confesses, but only in fragments.
ending_states.victory_stinger_primary: Not enough to bury Meridian. Plenty to frighten it.
victory_stinger_alternates: The archive coughs up truth in damaged packets.
You force a confession with pages missing.
The lie breaks first. The paperwork catches up later.
ending_states.ending_title: A Testimony With Holes In It
ending_states.ending_subtitle: You got the truth out. Meridian made sure some of it stayed expensive.
body_paragraphs: The concealment layer tears, but not evenly. Enough lies die to prove the shape of the crime. Not enough evidence survives to pin every hand to every signature. Applause cuts out in patches. Some screens relabel harm correctly. Others keep smiling through it like trained parasites. Meridian Spire starts telling on itself, then remembers there are still rooms it taught to swallow their own receipts.

The survivors who hear the broken confession understand more than leadership wanted and less than the dead deserved. Still, partial truth is not a small thing here. It is a weapon, a rumor, a floor-plan scribbled in blood and toner. You do not end the company. You make it expensive for the company to keep pretending it never knew.
ending_states.archive_header: Final Record: Confirmed Harm, Incomplete Chain
ending_states.archive_summary: Root Access was forced open, but one or more Everrise proofs were missing. The archive confirms systemic harm and executive deception, yet parts of the chain of authorship remain obscured.
ending_states.archive_closing_line: Enough truth escaped to ruin morale decks for years.

ending_states.title: Licensed Survival
ending_states.trigger_hint: Use when the player survives the final confrontation with some proof in hand, but relies on recognized authority to pass Root Access and does not fully rupture concealment.
ending_states.clear_banner: You get out with evidence and a compromised signature.
ending_states.victory_stinger_primary: Meridian let you live because it misread what you were carrying.
victory_stinger_alternates: A partial record with executive fingerprints is still a bomb.
The system approved its own injury request.
You leave with enough proof to make meetings quieter.
ending_states.ending_subtitle: The company still has cover. It has less of it now.
body_paragraphs: You clear the tower without giving it the full public seizure it deserves. Root Access accepts the wrong kind of person because Meridian never learned the difference between rank and worth. That gets you through, gets you out, and gets some of the truth into the archive, but the final record lands like a controlled leak instead of a detonation.

This is the ending where leadership still tries to call the aftermath a contained issue. The problem for them is that containment now has names attached to it. You carry enough proof to spoil investor language, enough detail to make internal denials sweat, and enough survival left in you to keep being terribly inconvenient.
ending_states.archive_header: Final Record: Contained Lie, Escalating Leak
ending_states.archive_summary: The clear preserves evidence but not total exposure. The archive can connect major harms and executive knowledge, yet some concealment remains intact because Root Access was entered on the tower's terms.
ending_states.archive_closing_line: Not clean. Not complete. Still lethal to the official story.

ending_states.title: Quiet Survival
ending_states.trigger_hint: Use when the player clears the run but leaves Root Access untouched, regardless of proof count.
ending_states.clear_banner: You live first. The testimony comes later, if it comes at all.
ending_states.victory_stinger_primary: The tower keeps its deepest file. You keep breathing.
victory_stinger_alternates: Some survivors leave with evidence. Some leave with enough strength to come back angry.
You deny Meridian one more body and call that a start.
The archive records the win anyway, even if the building keeps the ugliest page.
ending_states.ending_subtitle: A successful clear does not always arrive with a clean confession attached.
body_paragraphs: You leave Root Access pulsing in the floor like an unopened threat. Maybe you are too hurt. Maybe the timing is bad. Maybe survival finally wins the argument against revelation, which is not cowardice no matter what management would call it. Meridian keeps its deepest language to itself for one more cycle, and you keep the one thing the tower was absolutely willing to spend: your life.

This ending is quieter, but it is not nothing. A survivor walking out with even a fragment of the truth is still a systems problem for Crown Meridian. The archive marks what you saw, what you beat, and what remains sealed. Later, when someone finally tears that last cover off the machine, your record will be waiting there like a witness who never stopped taking notes.
ending_states.archive_header: Final Record: Survivor Exit, Truth Deferred
ending_states.archive_summary: The run clears without a Root Access breach. The archive preserves the floor path, bosses, and any recovered proofs, but the deepest executive confession remains sealed for now.
ending_states.archive_closing_line: Meridian counts this as a retention success. The archive disagrees.

archive_recap_variants.panel_subtitle: All three proofs align. Root Access was forced.
archive_recap_variants.panel_body: This run leaves Meridian with no clean language left to hide behind. Assignment, casualty approval, and concealment failure all reached the same record. The archive can now describe the tower as intended harm operating at executive scale.

archive_recap_variants.panel_title: Archive Recap: Permission Became the Wound
archive_recap_variants.panel_subtitle: All three proofs align. Root Access was authenticated, then abused correctly.
archive_recap_variants.panel_body: The archive reaches a full case file, but the route matters: Meridian's own authority stack was used to open the door. This recap should feel elegant, criminal, and deeply embarrassing for the company.

archive_recap_variants.panel_title: Archive Recap: The Confession Came Out Damaged
archive_recap_variants.panel_subtitle: Root Access was forced with missing proof coverage.
archive_recap_variants.panel_body: Enough was recovered to confirm systemic harm and executive knowledge. Not enough survived to complete the full authorship chain. The archive should sound angry, useful, and unfinished.

archive_recap_variants.panel_title: Archive Recap: The Leak Has a Badge
archive_recap_variants.panel_subtitle: Some proof survived. The breach still moved through recognized access.
archive_recap_variants.panel_body: This clear leaves Meridian exposed but not stripped bare. The archive should emphasize that the player carried out enough evidence to poison the official version without yet burning it to the root.

archive_recap_variants.panel_title: Archive Recap: Survival Logged, Core Truth Pending
archive_recap_variants.panel_subtitle: The run cleared without opening Root Access.
archive_recap_variants.panel_body: The archive records the clear, the bosses, and any proofs recovered, but the deepest executive confession remains sealed. This variant should value survival without pretending survival and revelation are the same achievement.
